breaking.title: Russian attacks kill five in Ukraine, local officials say
breaking.summary: Strikes on the southeastern city of Zaporizhzhia killed two people and injured ‌16, Regional Governor Ivan Fedorov said

breaking.title: Oil climbs following renewed US, Iran strikes in Middle East
breaking.summary: Oil prices saw an uptick as renewed US-Iran tensions in the Middle East disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Despite reports of a potential de-escalation and renewed talks, analysts caution that a full recovery of Gulf oil supply could take months due to lingering issues. Saudi Aramco has resumed loadings, but a recent helicopter crash adds to the complex situation.

breaking.title: Pakistan says it carried out ground operation, strikes along Afghan border; 29 militants killed
breaking.summary: Pakistani forces carried out “calibrated strikes” against terrorist hideouts and safe havens in the border region

breaking.title: Europe's heatwave linked to 1,300 deaths, WHO says, as Germany hits record 41.7C
breaking.summary: WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus warned that Europe is not prepared for high temperatures.

breaking.title: Two boys pulled from Venezuela earthquake rubble among 33 people rescued over weekend
breaking.summary: With tens of thousands of people missing, relatives face another night waiting for news of loved ones as the crucial window for locating survivors closes.

breaking.title: Israel destroys Hezbollah underground infrastructure in southern Lebanon
breaking.summary: The U.S. was informed ahead of the attack, which targeted a 200-meter (656-ft)-long tunnel in the ‌town of Majdal Zoun, according to the statement from Israeli PM Netanyahu ‌and Defence Minister Israel Katz

breaking.title: Bihar on verge of bankruptcy, says Tejashwi
breaking.summary: While addressing the press at the party office, the Rashtriya Janata Dal (RJD) leader alleged that the state is facing extreme financial loss due to scams, corruption, revenue loss, widening fiscal deficit and mounting debt
